medications.indication_rationale: Topical antifungal to and beyond the border; continue past clearance.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply twice daily (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Given the severity, extent and steroid modification, a short oral course ensures eradication.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 250 mg orally daily for ~2 weeks (as per source note; per prescriber)

medications.drug_name: potent topical corticosteroid on the groin
medications.indication_rationale: Drives tinea incognito and causes atrophy — must be stopped.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop the strong steroid, dry the groin completely with a separate towel after showering, wear loose cotton underwear, and treat any athlete's foot to prevent re-inoculation.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Removes moisture/occlusion and the steroid driver, and stops re-seeding from the feet.

patient_education_points.point: This is 'jock itch' — a groin fungal infection that the strong steroid cream supercharged. Stop that cream. We treat it with an antifungal cream plus a short course of antifungal tablets to clear it inside and out.
Keep the groin dry (separate towel), wear loose cotton underwear, and treat your athlete's foot so it doesn't reinfect the groin. The brown discolouration takes months to fade.
If the area becomes hot, hard and spreading, oozes thick smelly pus, or you develop a fever — seek medical care.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Resolution and any steroid-induced skin thinning.

must_include_items: Diagnose tinea cruris (scrotal sparing, scaly advancing border) vs candida/erythrasma and STOP the potent steroid
Topical + short oral antifungal (given severity/steroid modification)
Drying/barrier + loose cotton underwear + treat concurrent tinea pedis
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires stopping the steroid + topical (± short oral) antifungal + drying/tinea-pedis measures, with a secondary-infection safety-net.
